semantic.role: Infrastructure utility class for atomic filesystem operations. Provides safe file write and file copy operations with temporary file staging and directory creation.
responsibilities: Atomic file write: writes content to a temporary file and renames to target path, preventing corruption from partial writes.
Automatic directory creation: creates parent directories if they do not exist before writing.
Unique temporary file naming: uses cryptographic randomness (crypto.randomBytes) to generate temp file names.
File copy operation: provides a method to copy a file from source to destination (inferred from fs.copyFileSync usage).
Cleanup of stale temporary files: possibly removes leftover temp files (fs.unlinkSync usage).
outOfScope: Configuration management or environment variable handling.
User interfaces or CLI command processing.
Higher-level orchestration (e.g., pipeline execution, scanning, LLM calls).
invariants: File writes are atomic: the target file is either completely updated or untouched if the process is interrupted.
All parent directories required for a file path are created before any write.
Temporary files are always cleaned up after a successful write.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a reliable, atomic file I/O utility for the entire ArchSpine system to prevent data corruption during writes.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: No specific recent change information from git history; file appears to be part of the initial v1.0.0 architectural governance foundation.

publicSurface.description: Class exposing static methods for atomic filesystem operations. Consumed by infrastructure modules that need safe write or copy capabilities.
Writes content to a file atomically by writing to a temporary file and then renaming it. Used to ensure data integrity during scans, syncs, and artifact generation.

English.role: Infrastructure utility class for atomic filesystem operations. Provides safe file write and file copy operations with temporary file staging and directory creation.
responsibilities: Safely writes file content by first writing to a temporary file, then renaming it to the target. This makes the operation atomic and prevents file corruption from interrupted processes.
Automatically creates parent directories if they do not exist when writing a file.
Generates unique temporary filenames using cryptographic random bytes to avoid collisions.
Offers a file copy operation that copies a source file to a destination path.
Cleans up stale or leftover temporary files as needed.
outOfScope: Configuration management or environment variable handling.
User interface commands or CLI handling.
Orchestration of higher-level tasks like scanning, pipeline execution, or LLM interactions.
